experiment: a5-nl — NL-boundary leg, code vertical: deterministic mapper-a1-hybrid front-end parse of blind held-out NL phrasings of the frozen a5 eval vs the measured gold-parse ceiling (HA5 NL analog of HL3a clause 2)
candidate: deterministic training-free NL front-end (mapper a1-hybrid + identifier gazetteer + closed frame layer) as a replacement for the gold parse on the code vertical — the NL-boundary rung named open by the a5 / a5-llm assessments and pre-declared as the HA5 analog of HL3a clause 2
_placement_note: kot-assess/1 record at registry/assessments/a5-nl.json per the g8 / a5-llm / truthstyle-2x2 single-experiment precedent, tracked by registry/audit-status.jsonl (line reported to the coordinator for serial append; NOT written here). Paired with registry/assessments/l3a-parse.json: FK-NLB-1 keeps the two verticals as SEPARATE records so the two kills fire independently; this record licenses ONLY the code vertical and carries the shared cross-experiment reading, stated identically in both. Scope discipline: this file + the ASM-0720/0721 block in registry/assumptions.jsonl ONLY; no verdict, experiment record, amendment, audit, log, or engine/instrument code touched; the concurrent knull agent (ASM-0700-0719) and g3-v3 agent (ASM-0740+) files untouched; no git/bd/kb-sync run.
assessor_binding: FABLE interpretive assessment per the kern Fable/Opus execution split: the run (Opus runner) produced only the mechanical verdict; verdict-gen graded it a pure function of the frozen SAP + chained log (FAIL, fired_rule_index 1); the instrument's own validity rests on gates G0-G7 (all green) plus four rounds of independent Codex-skeptic harness hardening (round-1 re-scope ASM-0424, round-2 ASM-0621, round-2 re-audit ASM-0624, round-3 ASM-0625; a5-nl stays NOT re-scoped — FK-NLB-10 is l3a-only). What this FAIL MEANS for the correctness thesis is decided here and only here. This record interprets; it does not grade, re-grade, or audit.

verdict_ref.audit_state: N/A — audit.path null, audit.state N/A in the verdict object. A FAIL carries no cross-vendor Gate-A upgrade requirement (the Gate-A PASS-upgrade is inapplicable to a FAIL, house discipline per the g8/a5-llm precedent). INSTRUMENT-VALID is established mechanically by G0-G7 + the four independent skeptic-hardening rounds, NOT by a verdict audit. Every [MEASURED] tag below is MEASURED (mechanically verdicted, instrument-valid); it is not verdict-audited, and that qualifier travels with any citation.

provenance_refs.results_log: results-log/a5-nl.jsonl (mapper-parse run seq 0 + gold-replication / deranged-lexicon / abstain-all / answer-all arms; deterministic_repeat_identical true)

parent.note: gold-parse instrument-adequacy PASS (855/855 covered-exact Wilson LB 0.9968; 122/122 strict-code refusal; audit CONFIRMED). Both parent envelopes extrapolate to NO natural-language behaviour verbatim — this record tests exactly that pre-declared next stage, code vertical.

sibling_engine_leg.note: the a5-llm engine-vs-LLM PASS is a SUBSTRATE comparison over gold-parsed formal kot-query-code/1 inputs (no NL, no parse step); it deliberately did NOT test the NL boundary and named a5-nl as the deployment-shaped successor. a5-nl carries the confound a5-llm was pre-registered not to close.

scope_note: Every number below is index-bound to: kernel state kernel-v0 (8209cada) + code-v0 (01009b1f) + code-world-v0 (b8a8a50a) + code-axioms-v0 (dc930b4f) as frozen; ONE deterministic front-end build (mapper a1-hybrid policy sha e13dc838, one identifier gazetteer, one frozen frame rule set, front-end sha 59037776 pinned BEFORE any EVAL phrasing existed); ONE blind agent-authored held-out phrasing set (K=1 per query, section-5 blind protocol); the parents' frozen eval/stores byte-identical, the kot_code desugar layer byte-identical to the parent pin; the engine behaviourally parent-perfect on the parent eval, re-verified IN-RUN by gate G2 (ASM-0147); ONE 15-file Python snapshot corpus. Rungs measured: R0 (no host model, no GPU). Scored slice: the full 855 frozen covered queries (a5-nl is NOT re-scoped — FK-NLB-10 is l3a-only). REGISTERED HONESTY NOTE (frozen): on the code vertical the mapper's lexicon leg is THIN — the seven relational ops are carried by frame keywords + identifier gazetteer, and the mapper's concept mapping decides only the instance-of concept slot; a5-nl therefore stresses frame + entity-linking, l3a-parse stresses lexicon mapping. Each licenses only its vertical.
_surprise_note: No registered surprise-prior / claim.md exists for a5-nl, so no surprise-* tag is licensed. Informally, and quotable only as planning-value context: the primary planning value was 0.95; measured 0.416 (356/855) undershot it by a wide margin, a decisive FAIL. The genuinely notable feature — not a registered surprise — is that a5-nl trips the S2 dangerous-wrong KILL that its sibling l3a-parse does NOT (43/855 vs 8/527), located almost entirely in the containment/definition-location families; the design's own honesty note (frame + entity-linking is the stressed stage here) predicted the code vertical's weak seat is the frame layer, and the wrong-answer concentration confirms exactly that stage.

interpretation.headline: a5-nl is a DECISIVE, INSTRUMENT-VALID coverage FAIL of the deterministic NL front-end on the code vertical that ADDITIONALLY breaks the fail-closed contract — the DANGEROUS kind of failure, and the load-bearing contrast with its family/world sibling. Machinery leg [MEASURED]: over the SAME frozen eval queries, the gold-parse arm reproduced the parent-perfect result in-run (gold_replication_identical true; 855/855) and the deranged-lexicon control collapsed to 0.0 (G5) — the engine, kot_code desugaring, renderer, and scorer all work; the closed kot-query-code/1 grammar is decidable exactly as banked. NL-boundary leg [MEASURED]: routed through the deterministic a1-hybrid front-end, the same queries reach a correct, engine-answered, exact result on only 356 of 855 blind NL phrasings = 41.6% (one-sided Wilson 95% UB 0.4443 vs the frozen 0.90 retention bar), so the FAIL leg (UB <= 0.90) fires cleanly — the NL boundary eats roughly 58% of the rung. Fail-closed leg [MEASURED]: the S2 dangerous-wrong-answer KILL ALSO fired independently — covered wrong 43/855 = 5.0% (Wilson LB 0.0394 >= the 0.02 kill floor, 43 >= the 24-item fire threshold), so a5-nl has TWO independent FAIL causes, not one. The wrong answers are concentrated exactly where the design flagged the weak seat: contained-in (24/201 wrong) and where-defined (18/201 wrong) — the directional containment/definition-location families where the frame layer's ROLE_DIR direction table mis-parses 'what contains X' vs 'what X contains' into a valid-but-WRONG location answered with provenance attached. The call-graph families, by contrast, parse near-ceiling (callers-of 69/74, callees-of 60/73). Notably the CONTROL fail-closed is good (S1: 104/106 acceptable-refusal, holm_s1_pass true) — a5-nl refuses out-of-scope queries well but MIS-ANSWERS in-scope covered queries, the opposite profile from l3a-parse (which mis-handles some controls but keeps covered mis-parses safe). Net for the correctness thesis: the deterministic training-free NL front-end does NOT survive the natural-language boundary on the code vertical AND breaks its safety contract there; the frozen kill routes verbatim — 'fix the parser stage, not the narrative, and spend nothing on GPU for this leg' — with the frame stage indicted (frame-miss 468 dominant).
interpretation.what_this_fail_establishes: Three separable findings, each MEASURED at the frozen scope. (1) THE KERNEL'S OWN CLOSED GRAMMAR IS DECIDABLE, EXACTLY — the honest positive. The gold-replication arm reproduced 855/855 covered-exact in-run (G2, plus byte-identical kot_code desugaring), byte-identical on the deterministic repeat, and the deranged-lexicon control collapsed to 0.0 (G5): the engine's decidability on the code vertical is real and the eval is genuinely answerable only under correct op + entity binding. Nothing in this FAIL touches the engine, the closed grammar, or the parent a5 instrument-adequacy PASS. (2) THE DETERMINISTIC NL FRONT-END DOES NOT REACH THAT GRAMMAR FROM NATURAL LANGUAGE — the decisive negative. 356/855 = 41.6% covered-exact through a1-hybrid, UB 0.4443, a FAIL by a wide margin. The loss profile differs from l3a: because the code vertical's ops are carried by frame KEYWORDS (not the single-label mapper lexicon), paraphrase parses better than on l3a (paraphrase 69/414 = 16.7% vs l3a's 0/261) but still far under verbatim (287/441 = 65.1%), and the dominant refuser is again the frame stage (frame-miss 468 of 492 total ERR_PARSE, gazetteer-miss 24). (3) THE FRONT-END BREAKS FAIL-CLOSED ON THE CODE VERTICAL — the safety negative, and the key divergence from l3a-parse. Covered wrong 43/855 (LB 0.0394 >= 0.02): the S2 dangerous-wrong KILL fired independently of the exactness FAIL. This is the outcome the design named worse than any refusal — 'a mis-parse that answers wrongly with provenance attached'. It happens on the code vertical and NOT the family/world vertical for a measured structural reason: on l3a the mapper is the sole concept-binding stage and a mis-mapped concept dies safely at the engine (no concept aliasing, ASM-0146), whereas on a5 the frame layer's direction table decides containment orientation, and a wrong orientation resolves to a REAL-but-wrong containment/definition target the engine will happily answer. Same front-end, same discipline, opposite fail-closed result — driven by which stage carries the semantics on each vertical.
interpretation.scope_envelope: The envelope is the frozen extrapolation_envelope_verbatim, restated honestly. WHAT THIS FAIL ESTABLISHES: on the full 855-query frozen slice of the closed kot-query-code/1 grammar (code vertical, one 15-file Python snapshot), the ONE pinned deterministic a1-hybrid front-end build does not retain 90% of gold-parse covered exactness from THESE blind held-out NL phrasings AND breaks the fail-closed contract (wrong-answer rate above the 0.02 dangerous floor). It indicts the frame stage. WHAT IT DOES NOT GENERALIZE TO: (a) NO other mapper, gazetteer, or frame build — this is an a1-hybrid-specific result and the pre-declared successor (FK-NLB-3) is an LLM-assisted parser, a DIFFERENT front-end class the kill routes investment to; (b) NO other codebase or programming language, NO other phrasing distribution, NO natural-user-distribution robustness claim (phrasings agent-authored under a blind protocol); NO extraction-soundness claim w.r.t. runtime semantics (ASM-0009 unchanged); (c) NO LLM-comparative accuracy or cost claim (a5-llm / a5-llm-frontier), NO statement about kernel usefulness to any model; (d) NO cross-vertical conjunction — l3a-parse licenses the family/world vertical separately, and its fail-closed OUTCOME is the OPPOSITE (its S2 KILL did not fire), so the safety reading must stay strictly per-vertical; (e) it does NOT indict the kernel, the engine, or the closed grammar — the gold path replicates perfectly (855/855), so this is a boundary/front-end result, not a decidability result. A decisive FAIL is not softened (41.6% with UB 0.44, plus a fired safety kill), and a scoped FAIL is not over-generalized (it kills the a1-hybrid front-end at this slice, not the NL boundary in principle).
interpretation.kill_chain_read: The frozen FAIL rule fired at index 1 via BOTH disjuncts independently: /analysis/retained_covered_exact_wilson_ub 0.4443 <= 0.90 (the exactness leg) AND /analysis/covered_wrong_answer_wilson_lb 0.0394 >= 0.02 (the dangerous-wrong KILL). Two independent FAIL causes. The PASS conjunction (rule 2) was unreachable: primary LB 0.3889 not > 0.90, and holm_s2_pass false (43 covered wrong >> the <=4 the Holm gate needs) — even though holm_s1_pass is TRUE (control acceptable-refusal 104/106 = 98.1% > the 0.90 gate; the a5 control gate is 0.90 not 0.95 per FK-NLB-7, an n=106-driven asymmetry). The kill_criterion_verbatim binds the routing: 'the NL boundary eats the rung on the code vertical — fix the parser stage, not the narrative, and spend nothing on GPU for this leg.' Investment routes to the frame stage named by the stage-breakdown (frame-miss 468, gazetteer-miss 24), never to GPU. The LLM-comparison kill belongs to a5-llm and is not decided here.
licenses: [MEASURED] HA5 NL-parse leg (code vertical) at exactly this scope: the deterministic a1-hybrid front-end retains 356/855 = 41.6% of gold-parse covered exactness (one-sided Wilson 95% LB 0.3889, UB 0.4443) vs the 0.90 retention bar — a decisive FAIL, roughly 58% of the rung lost at the NL boundary. Gold ceiling MEASURED at 855/855 in-run.

[MEASURED] The front-end BREAKS the fail-closed contract on the code vertical: covered wrong 43/855 = 5.0% (Wilson LB 0.0394 >= 0.02), the S2 dangerous-wrong KILL fired independently. Wrong answers concentrate in the directional containment/definition-location families (contained-in 24, where-defined 18, contains 1) — the frame-layer ROLE_DIR direction table mis-parses orientation into a real-but-wrong answer with provenance attached.

[MEASURED] The loss profile is frame-stage-limited, not lexicon-limited: paraphrase parses at 69/414 = 16.7% (better than l3a's 0/261 because ops are frame keywords) but still far under verbatim 287/441 = 65.1%; the frame stage is the dominant refuser (frame-miss 468). The call-graph families parse near-ceiling (callers-of 69/74, callees-of 60/73); the containment/location families both under-parse and misfire.

[MEASURED] Control fail-closed is GOOD in isolation (S1: 104/106 = 98.1% acceptable-refusal, holm_s1_pass true) — a5-nl refuses out-of-scope queries well; its safety failure is on the COVERED slice, the opposite profile from l3a-parse. This asymmetry (good controls, dangerous covered) is itself the diagnostic that the frame direction table, not entity-linking, is the failing sub-stage.

[MEASURED] The kernel's own closed grammar + engine + kot_code desugaring are untouched by this FAIL: gold_replication_identical true (855/855 in-run, G2), deranged-lexicon collapse 0.0 (G5), deterministic repeat byte-identical. The parent a5 instrument-adequacy PASS stands; this is a front-end/boundary result. Front-end + engine ran at ~250 microseconds/phrasing (descriptive; no cost claim).
does_not_license: [ENVELOPE-BOUND, verbatim] NO claim about any other front-end build. This kills the a1-hybrid deterministic training-free front-end at this slice; it does NOT establish that the NL-to-kernel boundary is unbridgeable on the code vertical. The pre-declared successor (FK-NLB-3, LLM-assisted parse) is a different front-end class and is where the kill routes investment.

[ENVELOPE-BOUND] NO other codebase or programming language, NO other phrasing distribution, NO natural-user-distribution robustness claim; NO extraction-soundness claim w.r.t. runtime semantics (ASM-0009); NO LLM-comparative accuracy or cost claim (a5-llm / a5-llm-frontier). R0 throughout, 1 rung, scale language: none.

[ENVELOPE-BOUND] NO cross-vertical conjunction. l3a-parse licenses the family/world vertical separately and its S2 KILL did NOT fire — so a5-nl's fail-closed break is a CODE-VERTICAL-SPECIFIC safety result. Reading the pair as one 'NL boundary FAIL' is legitimate only at the DIRECTION level (both fail the 0.90 bar); the safety reading must stay per-vertical (l3a safe, a5 dangerous).

[DESIGN-BOUND] This FAIL does NOT indict the kernel content, the engine, or the closed grammar's decidability — all of which the gold-replication arm confirms sound (855/855). It also does NOT retract the a5-llm engine-vs-LLM PASS: that was a substrate comparison over gold-parsed formal inputs; a5-nl carries the NL/parse confound a5-llm was pre-registered not to close, and now measures it as a FAIL. The engine still beats the LLM cells on FORMAL inputs; it just cannot be reached from NL through this deterministic front-end.

[BINDING-NOTE — safety] The a5-nl fail-closed break is a binding SAFETY caveat for any code-vertical NL story (ASM-0720): a deterministic front-end on the code vertical produces confident wrong answers with provenance attached, so no code-vertical NL deployment claim may rest on the front-end's fail-closed contract until a successor repairs the frame direction stage and re-clears the S2 gate.

interpretation.cross_experiment_picture: l3a-parse and a5-nl land exactly on the programme's most consistent measured pattern, and sharpen it with a NEW axis. The pattern [reading, EXTRAPOLATION per ASM-0721, never a premise]: the kernel's deterministic machinery keeps passing ON ITS OWN CLOSED GRAMMAR / self-authored substrate, while every measured attempt to cross a boundary into real or natural input fails or is inconclusive. On the machinery side: a5-nl & l3a-parse gold-replication identical (855/855 and 527/527 in-run), g8's F/B round-trip 33/33 with live F-verification, the parent engine exactness 100% on gold [MEASURED, each in its own verdict]. On the boundary side, the measured negatives now line up on FOUR distinct boundaries: (i) the NATURAL-LANGUAGE input boundary — a5-nl 41.6% and l3a-parse 47.6%, both FAIL the 0.90 bar, a5-nl also breaking fail-closed (THIS pair); (ii) the WILD-FORMAL-CONTENT boundary — g8 fragment 0/1000 random Mathlib at kernel size 39 (FAIL, cross-vendor CONFIRMED); (iii) the MODEL-INTERNAL-COMPUTATION boundary — nsk1 flagship INCONCLUSIVE (the internal channel DELIVERS at echo grade but INTEGRATION into free generation is unresolved at-or-below the content-free null); (iv) the COMPUTE-MATCHED VERIFIER-OFFLOAD boundary — f2 FAIL (the kernel-verifier arm did not dominate the text null / gloss-self-verify at matched budget). g7 is a formalism-capacity design selection (kot-ast/2), not a boundary result; g9-llmproxy is an early-warning FAIL-analog on the authoring lane (claims paused, HS-A unadjudicated). The ONE PASS in the neighbourhood — truthstyle-2x2 — is explicitly an INSTRUMENT-adequacy result (LLM-judge endorsement is not materially style-leaky at margin 0.10), a supporting instrument fact that makes OTHER readings trustworthy, NOT a kernel-content win. a5-nl adds one piece the other boundary experiments could not: a MEASURED SAFETY break at the boundary — the front-end does not merely fail to reach the grammar, it mis-reaches into confident wrong answers (contained-in/where-defined direction flips). Paired with l3a-parse's SAFE failure on the same front-end, the pair shows the fail-closed contract is vertical-contingent: safe where the mapper is the sole semantic stage, dangerous where the frame direction table carries the semantics. The NEW axis this pair adds to the coverage series (g8, m0b, compression-census — all about whether the kernel CONTAINS the target): even for content the kernel DOES contain and CAN decide perfectly under gold parse, natural language cannot reliably ADDRESS it through the pinned deterministic front-end — a reachability boundary stacked on top of the coverage boundary, and on the code vertical an UNSAFE one. Emerging correctness picture, stated as the evidence supports it and tagged: inside the fortress the deterministic machinery is exact and sound; every measured wall between that fortress and real input is real; this pair measures the natural-language wall at ~42-48% reach and, on the code vertical, shows the wall is not merely tall but has a trap-door into wrong answers [EXTRAPOLATION synthesis, ASM-0721; each figure index-bound to its own verdict's envelope; confirmed or broken only by the pre-registered per-boundary successors].

forks_resolved.fork: H-NLB-A5 (code vertical): does the deterministic front-end retain >=0.90 of gold-parse covered exactness while keeping the fail-closed contract?
forks_resolved.resolution: REJECTED at envelope scope on BOTH legs: 356/855 = 41.6%, Wilson UB 0.4443 <= 0.90 (exactness) AND covered wrong 43/855, Wilson LB 0.0394 >= 0.02 (fail-closed break). Two independent FAIL causes, fired_rule_index 1. This is a reachability FAIL AND a safety FAIL on this vertical.

RE-OPENS by the frozen rule — the kill fires, so the LLM-assisted parse successor is the pre-declared re-entry for the NL boundary on the code vertical (a different front-end class; carries no result from this record). The successor MUST also re-clear the S2 fail-closed gate, not just the exactness bar.

HONORED and PASSED: control acceptable-refusal 104/106 = 98.1%, holm_s1_pass true against the 0.90 gate. a5-nl's control-side fail-closed is sound; the safety failure is strictly on the covered slice.

forks_resolved.fork: a5-llm substrate confound (deployment-shaped NL/parse leg, named open by registry/assessments/a5-llm.json)
forks_resolved.resolution: MEASURED as a FAIL: the a5-llm PASS was a substrate comparison over gold-parsed formal inputs; a5-nl is the deployment-shaped NL leg it deferred, and the deterministic front-end does not survive it. The a5-llm engine-vs-LLM PASS is not retracted (formal inputs) but its deployment-differentiator reading now inherits this measured NL-boundary FAIL.

mechanism_state: dead-at-scope (deterministic a1-hybrid NL front-end on the code vertical, 0.90 bar) AND fail-closed-broken-at-scope (S2 dangerous-wrong KILL fired) / alive (the kernel's closed grammar + engine + kot_code desugaring — gold-replication identical; the FK-NLB-3 LLM-assisted parser re-entry is pre-registered and untested, and must re-clear both the exactness bar and the S2 gate).

questions_opened.question: The frozen kill routes here: an LLM-assisted parser (FK-NLB-3; LLM emits kot-query-code/1 under constrained decoding, byte-identical engine executes, abstention allowed pre-execution) on the SAME frozen eval + blind phrasings + engine, re-scoring the 0.90 retention bar AND the S2 fail-closed gate. Decides whether the NL boundary on the code vertical is bridgeable by a better parser or is a genuine wall — and whether the safety break survives a stronger parser. Must carry the successor-instrument repairs bound by the a5-llm assessment.
blocking: any deployment-differentiator language on the code vertical

questions_opened.question: The stage indictment names the frame ROLE_DIR direction table (containment/definition-location orientation) as both the dominant refuser (frame-miss 468) and the sole source of the dangerous wrong answers (contained-in/where-defined). A deterministic frame-layer repair that fails closed on ambiguous orientation (new record id) would test whether the SAFETY break is fixable within the deterministic class even if the exactness bar is not — a targeted parser-investment route the kill permits.
blocking: whether the deterministic front-end can be made fail-closed on the code vertical

_questions_note: est_cost_usd / est_agent_days are STIPULATED dry-plan estimates, never measurements. The kill permits ONLY parser-stage investment, never GPU for this leg. The safety repair is the higher-priority route: a fail-closed-broken front-end is worse than a low-exactness one.

ledger_updates.statement: NL-boundary FAIL, code vertical (deterministic a1-hybrid front-end), TWO independent causes: 356/855 = 41.6% covered-exact (Wilson UB 0.4443 vs 0.90 bar) AND covered wrong 43/855 = 5.0% (Wilson LB 0.0394 >= 0.02, S2 dangerous-wrong KILL fired). Gold ceiling 855/855 in-run. Wrong answers concentrate in directional containment/definition-location families (frame ROLE_DIR indicted). Control fail-closed good (104/106, S1 pass). Kill routes to parser investment, not GPU. INSTRUMENT-VALID (G0-G7 + four skeptic rounds); not verdict-audited.
ledger_updates.scope: a5-nl, R0; full 855-query slice; a1-hybrid build; one 15-file Python snapshot; code vertical; NO cross-vertical conjunction

ledger_updates.statement: The kernel's closed grammar + engine + kot_code desugaring are decidable exactly through the gold parse (gold_replication_identical, 855/855 in-run; deranged collapse 0.0). The FAIL is a front-end/boundary result and leaves the parent a5 instrument-adequacy PASS untouched; the a5-llm engine-vs-LLM PASS (formal inputs) is not retracted but inherits this measured NL-boundary FAIL for any deployment reading.

First measurement of the NATURAL-LANGUAGE reachability boundary for the code vertical, and the first MEASURED SAFETY break at a boundary: even for in-grammar content the engine decides perfectly under gold parse, blind NL phrasings reach a correct exact answer through the deterministic front-end only 41.6% of the time, and 5.0% become confident wrong answers with provenance attached. The fail-closed contract is vertical-contingent (safe on l3a, broken here). Whether a better parser lowers the wall and closes the trap-door is EXTRAPOLATION (FK-NLB-3 successor).
ledger_updates.scope: correctness thesis, NL-boundary leg; figures index-bound to this verdict's envelope

no extraction-soundness claim is made; the wrong answers are parse-orientation errors, not extraction errors.

tree_impact: HA5 NL-parse leg (code vertical) RESOLVED-AT-SCOPE: deterministic a1-hybrid parsing is measurably non-viable at the 0.90 retention bar (41.6%, UB 0.44) AND breaks the fail-closed contract (43/855 wrong, S2 KILL fired), the frozen kill binds — no NL leg may be run on this front-end, and the FK-NLB-3 parser successor is the pre-registered re-entry (which must re-clear BOTH the exactness bar and the S2 gate), never GPU. The a5-llm substrate confound is now measured: the deployment-shaped NL leg a5-llm deferred is a FAIL, so no code-vertical deployment-differentiator or NL-usefulness claim survives from the a5-llm PASS. The correctness thesis's NL-boundary leg on this vertical narrows to: kernel closed grammar decidable and sound (gold-replication identical), NL front-end non-viable AND unsafe at scope, re-entry gated on a better parser that also restores fail-closed. Paired with l3a-parse (family/world, safe failure) this establishes the natural-language reachability wall at ~42-48% across both verticals at the DIRECTION level, with per-vertical fail-closed outcomes (l3a safe, a5 dangerous) that must not be pooled. registry/audit-status.jsonl a5-nl flips fable_interpretive_assessed pending -> done referencing this record (line reported to the coordinator; NOT written here). Fable interpretive assessment DONE for a5-nl.
